steps.title: Identify the task
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two flags. The notes give the key facts: El Salvador's flag has a palm branch (a plant) and Zambia's flag has an eagle (an animal). The correct choice must directly contrast these two flags, not merely mention both.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Vexillologists study flags, like those of El Salvador and Zambia.': names both flags but only makes a claim about vexillology, not any difference between the two flags. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Many countries feature symbols on their flags, such as animals, plants, or landforms, and the study of these designs is known as vexillology.': describes a general fact about flags worldwide; does not contrast El Salvador and Zambia specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Countries like El Salvador and Zambia use symbols on their flags; examples include plants and animals.': implies both countries share the same category of symbols without distinguishing which country has which type. ✗
  • Choice D — 'El Salvador includes a plant on its flag, whereas Zambia's flag includes an animal.': directly contrasts the two flags — plant vs. animal — using "whereas" to signal the opposition. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
